formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Accountancy - Computerised Accounting System in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Single Entry System",
    "formula": "Single entry system is the system of recording transactions in which all transactions are recorded on the credit side.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Single entry system is used by sole proprietors or small businesses."
  },
  {
    "name": "Double Entry System",
    "formula": "Double entry system is the system of recording transactions in which every transaction is recorded on both debit and credit sides.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Double entry system is used by all types of business organisations."
  },
  {
    "name": "Debit Note",
    "formula": "Debit note is a document that is used to record a decrease in asset or increase in liability.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Debit note is issued when goods are returned or when there is a decrease in asset or increase in liability."
  },
  {
    "name": "Credit Note",
    "formula": "Credit note is a document that is used to record an increase in asset or decrease in liability.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Credit note is issued when goods are supplied or when there is an increase in asset or decrease in liability."
  },
  {
    "name": "System Analysis",
    "formula": "System analysis is the process of evaluating the existing system and identifying its strengths and weaknesses.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "System analysis is a crucial step in the implementation of a computerised accounting system."
  },
  {
    "name": "System Design",
    "formula": "System design is the process of designing the new system based on the requirements identified during system analysis.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "System design involves identifying the inputs, processing, and outputs of the system."
  },
  {
    "name": "System Implementation",
    "formula": "System implementation is the process of installing and testing the new system.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "System implementation involves training the staff and ensuring that the system is operational."
  },
  {
    "name": "System Evaluation",
    "formula": "System evaluation is the process of evaluating the effectiveness of the new system.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "System evaluation involves identifying the strengths and weaknesses of the system and making necessary changes."
  },
  {
    "name": "Computerised Accounting System",
    "formula": "Computerised accounting system is an accounting system that uses computer hardware and software to process and store accounting data.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Computerised accounting system is used by all types of business organisations to improve efficiency and accuracy."
  },
  {
    "name": "Modules of Computerised Accounting System",
    "formula": "Modules of computerised accounting system include accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger, payroll, and inventory.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Each module of the computerised accounting system performs a specific function."
  },
  {
    "name": "Hardware Components",
    "formula": "Hardware components of computerised accounting system include CPU, memory, input/output devices, and storage devices.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Hardware components of the computerised accounting system perform basic functions."
  },
  {
    "name": "Software Components",
    "formula": "Software components of computerised accounting system include operating system, accounting software, and utility programs.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Software components of the computerised accounting system perform specific functions."
  }
]
```

Note that this formula sheet is based on the CBSE Class 11 Accountancy syllabus, and it may not include all the formulas and laws mentioned in the textbook.
